items.title: Train/validation/test splits
items.description: Hold-out partitions define where CV runs (often train+val) and where a final locked test set stays untouched. CV replaces a single validation slice when data is scarce or scores need averaging across folds.

items.title: Variance in generalization
items.description: Spread of fold scores estimates performance instability driven by variance—high std(m) across folds often indicates a high-variance model or sensitive split geometry.

items.title: Random forests
items.description: Tree depth, `min_samples_leaf`, and `max_features` are commonly compared using stratified CV metrics (AUC, accuracy) before locking a test-set score.

items.title: Ridge and Lasso
items.description: Regularization strength α is frequently chosen by CV over a log-spaced grid, comparing mean RMSE or MAE across folds for each penalty value.

items.title: Cross-validation for overfitting mitigation
items.description: The same k-fold and nested CV machinery appears when the primary goal is reducing overfitting or tuning with small data—see the Overfitting Mitigation topic for mitigation-first workflows.
